meta_title: For Founders: design with AI to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Design with AI to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no portfolio. An Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your MVP faster.
content.answer_block: Building a high-converting portfolio without history requires demonstrating 'ship-velocity' rather than just static design. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid UI scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative logic development, you can generate a professional, functional MVP in hours that proves your technical competency to potential clients and investors immediately.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the SaaS market is that nobody cares about your 'potential'—they care about your output. You are stuck in the infinite loop of needing a portfolio to get clients, but needing clients to build a portfolio. This catch-22 is the graveyard of thousands of promising founders who stall before they ever write a line of production code.

But the rules of the game have fundamentally changed. With AI-native development, you no longer need a decade of agency work to prove your worth. By utilizing high-velocity tooling like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can transform abstract concepts into tangible, revenue-ready products, effectively 'hacking' your way into a world-class portfolio overnight.

steps.title: Define the High-Value SaaS Niche
steps.description: Don't build a generic landing page. Focus on a specific B2B problem that requires a complex UI, such as a subscription dashboard or a SaaS analytics module.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a high-fidelity React component for a SaaS dashboard featuring a revenue analytics chart, a recent activity log, and a clean side navigation menu using Tailwind CSS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init 'Set up a Next.js project with Tailwind CSS and Shadcn UI components for a SaaS dashboard.'

steps.title: Scaffold the Core Layout with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the structural skeleton. This ensures your design follows modern best practices without manual CSS headaches.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive layout shell that includes a persistent sidebar, a top header with user profile dropdown, and a main content area with a grid system.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Generate the layout.tsx and components/sidebar.tsx files based on the Antigravity output.'

steps.title: Inject Business Logic via Claude Code
steps.description: A static site is just a picture. You need state management and API integration to show you can build real software.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a mock API service that fetches and displays revenue trends, user growth metrics, and customer churn rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Implement a mock data service in lib/api.ts and connect it to the dashboard components.'

steps.title: Refine Interaction Patterns
steps.description: Add polish through subtle animations and loading states to demonstrate attention to detail—the hallmark of a senior engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add Framer Motion animations to the dashboard cards and implement skeleton loaders for the data-fetching states.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Integrate Framer Motion and update components to show loading skeletons while fetching data.'

steps.title: Deploy & Document for Proof
steps.description: Your portfolio piece is only as good as its accessibility. Push to Vercel and write a README that explains the 'why' behind your design decisions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README.md that highlights the tech stack, the business problem solved, and the design philosophy used in this project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Generate a professional README.md file and prepare the project for a Vercel deployment.'

pro_tips: Focus on 'Micro-SaaS' modules rather than full-blown platforms; they are easier to sell as portfolio pieces.
Always include a 'Data Visualization' element; it implies technical complexity and backend integration skill.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your UI components; it proves you understand professional engineering standards.
Document your AI prompts in a 'Build Log'—showing how you use AI is a highly marketable skill in itself.
Optimize for mobile-first design; showing a responsive dashboard is the quickest way to pass the 'senior engineer' sniff test.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel, share the live link on X/LinkedIn with a breakdown of the business problem you solved, and start outreach to potential clients in that specific niche.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a founder. Your next client is looking for exactly what you can build right now. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
